meta.title: G60 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 5 Series G60 powertrain — B48, B58, B47, B57, 530e, 550e, M560e, i5 electric. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series G60 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G60 engine typically costs £2,500–£10,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on the powertrain — the four-cylinder petrol and diesel engines sit at £2,500–£5,000, the B58 six-cylinder at £4,500–£7,000, and the plug-in hybrids at £4,000–£10,000. Fitting adds £520–£1,170. Electric i5 models have no engine replacement cost — battery replacement is the equivalent major cost item. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which G60 powertrain is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30M2 (540i xDrive) is the G60's most reliable internal combustion engine — BMW's modern turbocharged straight-six is smooth, responsive, and genuinely robust. The B48 four-cylinder is also very reliable, and the B47 diesel is improved over the N47. The electric i5 powertrains are too new for long-term data but early reports are positive.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series G60?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 six-cylinder suffers from cooling system leaks and PCV valve failure. The B47 and B57 diesel engines can have EGR cooler leaks and DPF clogging with urban use. Plug-in hybrids (530e, 550e, M560e) face battery degradation over time — a costly out-of-warranty replacement. The i5 electric models are new but cooling system maintenance is critical.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series G60 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It's too early for definitive long-term reliability data, but the G60 uses proven engine families — the B48, B58, B47, and B57 are all well-established from previous generations. The B58 in particular has a strong reputation. The plug-in hybrids add complexity, and the i5 is new but represents BMW's latest EV technology. Overall, early indications are positive, but these are complex, high-tech vehicles.

mainEntity.name: Which G60 powertrain should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No powertrain outright — but if you're buying a plug-in hybrid, ensure the battery's state of health is verified. Battery replacement out of warranty is expensive (£6,000–£12,000). For diesel buyers, ensure the car has been used for regular motorway journeys to keep the DPF clear. Any powertrain without full service history is a risk.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G60 powertrain needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: For combustion engines: coolant loss or overheating (cooling system), rough idle or oil consumption (PCV valve), loss of power (DPF/EGR on diesel), or persistent warning lights. For PHEVs: reduced electric range (battery degradation). For i5: reduced range or charging issues (battery/cooling). Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a G60 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a £40,000 540i, a £4,500–£7,000 B58 recon is 11–18% — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Against a £35,000 520d, a £3,000–£5,000 B47 recon is 9–14% — a straightforward repair decision. These are high-value cars; the replacement cost is a manageable proportion of the vehicle's worth. For PHEVs, battery degradation is the main concern — if a battery replacement costs £6,000–£10,000 and the car is worth £30,000, it's 20–33% — still viable, but a decision to weigh carefully.

mainEntity.name: How long do G60 powertrains last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 and B48 can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The B47 and B57 diesels are similarly durable but require regular motorway driving to keep the DPF clear — 100,000+ miles is achievable. PHEV batteries are expected to last 8–10 years or 100,000 miles before significant degradation. The i5's battery is warranted for 8 years/100,000 miles; real-world longevity beyond that is unknown but likely similar to other EVs.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G60, G30, and G70?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G60 (2023–present) is the eighth-generation 5 Series, succeeding the G30 (2017–2023). The G60 is built on an updated CLAR platform and is significantly larger than its predecessor. The G70 is the seventh-generation 7 Series (2022–present) — a larger, more luxurious flagship that shares the CLAR platform but with a longer wheelbase and more upscale interior. The G60 and G70 share powertrains (B58, B57, electric) but the G70 offers a V8 (in the 760i) which the G60 no longer offers.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my G60 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block. For petrol: B48B20P (520i), B48B20O1 (530i), B58B30M2 (540i xDrive). For diesel: B47D20B (520d), B57D30 (540d xDrive). For PHEV: B48B20 (530e), B58B30 (550e xDrive), B58B30S (M560e xDrive). For electric: i5 eDrive40, i5 xDrive40, i5 M60 xDrive. Tell us your variant and registration year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every powertrain fitted to the G60 specifically — including 48V mild-hybrid petrol and diesel engines, plug-in hybrids, and the all-electric i5 range.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any G60 powertrain family. The B48 and B58 engines are well-established from earlier BMW generations and have proven themselves robust, with the B58 in particular being widely regarded as one of BMW's best modern engines. The B47 diesel is the improved successor to the N47, with timing chain issues largely resolved. The B57 six-cylinder diesel is also considered reliable. The plug-in hybrids add complexity but use proven engine bases. The electric i5 models represent BMW's latest EV technology and are too new for long-term reliability data; early reports are positive.

overview.intro: The G60 is the eighth generation of the BMW 5 Series, an executive saloon that began production in July 2023. Built on an updated version of the rear-wheel drive Cluster Architecture (CLAR) platform, shared with the larger G70 7 Series, it is significantly larger than its predecessor. Designed by Anders Thøgersen, the G60 introduces a bold new design language with a distinctive front grille and flush door handles. The engine line‑up is the most diverse in 5 Series history: 48V mild-hybrid four- and six-cylinder petrol and diesel engines, plug-in hybrids (530e, 550e xDrive, M560e xDrive), and the fully electric i5 range (eDrive40, xDrive40, M60 xDrive). The G60 is the last 5 Series generation to feature pure internal combustion engines, with the M560e effectively replacing the V8-powered M550i.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (updated), front-engine, rear-wheel drive / xDrive • Predecessor: BMW 5 Series (G30) • Successor: None currently • Sibling body styles in this generation: G61 Touring (estate), G68 long-wheelbase saloon
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The 5 Series nameplate generated significant UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], with demand for the G60 concentrated on the 520d diesel and 530e plug-in hybrid based on market patterns.

items.quote: The B48 four-cylinder delivers 258 bhp — more than enough for everyday driving — with excellent fuel economy and lower running costs than the six-cylinder. In the G60's lightweight CLAR platform, it's a genuinely capable executive saloon.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want modern performance with sensible running costs.

items.quote: The six-cylinder diesel is often overlooked in favour of the 530e or 540i, but it delivers 305 bhp of effortless torque with genuine 40+ mpg economy. It's the thinking buyer's choice — refined, powerful, and efficient.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder refinement with diesel economy.

economicsRule.title: The G60 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500–£7,000 B58 recon against a £35,000–£55,000 540i sits at 8–20% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold. For a 530e at £40,000–£50,000, a £4,000–£6,500 recon is 8–16% — a straightforward repair decision. These are high-value cars; the replacement cost is a manageable proportion of the vehicle's worth. But beware of battery degradation on PHEV and EV models — a battery replacement can cost £10,000+. Value the car and its battery health first; if it's a high-mileage PHEV without battery history, budget for potential degradation.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G60 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 520d (B47D20B) — proven diesel efficiency, low CO2, strong economy.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G60 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M560e xDrive (B58B30S) — complex PHEV system, expensive parts, high performance = high running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G60 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 530i (B48B20O1) — modern four-cylinder, strong performance, lower running costs than six-cylinder.

problems.title: B58 Cooling System & PCV Valve (540i, 550e, M560e)
problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system and PCV valve inspected at every major service — a £400–£1,000 preventative repair is cheaper than the £4,500+ replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. On a £40,000+ car, this is a manageable cost of ownership.

problems.title: B47/B57 Diesel EGR Cooler & DPF (520d, 540d)
problems.recommendation: If you're buying a diesel G60 for urban use, budget for DPF maintenance — £400–£1,200 for a clean or EGR cooler replacement is cheaper than the £3,000+ replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. Motorway driving helps keep the DPF clear.

problems.title: PHEV Battery Degradation (530e, 550e, M560e)
problems.recommendation: If you're buying a used G60 PHEV, check the battery's state of health — a battery with significant degradation will reduce the car's value and increase running costs. Budget for potential battery replacement if you plan to keep the car long-term. On a £40,000+ car, a £6,000–£10,000 battery replacement is a serious but manageable cost.

problems.title: i5 Battery & Cooling System (All i5 Models)
problems.recommendation: If you're buying an i5, ensure the battery cooling system has been serviced and the battery's state of health is verified. On a £50,000+ car, a £7,000–£12,000 battery replacement is a significant but manageable cost — but it's one you should budget for if keeping the car beyond the 8-year warranty period.
